description:
If you’re confused about which plan suits your family best, book a FREE call with Ditto and we’ll help you compare, choose, and buy in under 2 minutes: https://ditto.sh/vj9ov0 

HDFC ERGO Optima Secure – Is this health insurance plan actually worth it? In this video, we share a detailed HDFC ERGO Optima Secure review and explain how HDFC Optima Secure health insurance works. 

By the end, you’ll understand the key features, coverage benefits, and whether HDFC ERGO Optima Secure health insurance is the right choice for you.The HDFC Ergo Optima Secure plan is a flagship offering that’s become one of the top choices for families seeking reliable, feature-rich coverage in 2025.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
*Timestamps:* 
0:00  – Introduction of HDFC ERGO Optima Secure
0:58  – HDFC ERGO General Insurance   
2:12  – Core Features of HDFC ERGO Optima Secure
2:45  – Benefits of HDFC ERGO Optima Secure  
4:24  – Optional Add-ons of HDFC ERGO Optima Secure 
5:53  – Discounts on HDFC ERGO Optima Secure
6:19  – Premiums of HDFC ERGO Optima Secure
6:46  – Final Take on HDFC ERGO Optima Secure
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
*Doing your research? These detailed reviews will give you complete clarity:*
👉 Axis Max Life Term Insurance Plan Review : https://youtu.be/oixOhMiClVY?si=kRobXoETxDvf86p6
👉 HDFC Ergo Optima Secure vs Acko Health Insurance: https://youtu.be/50ONkuVQSsM?si=AbCg8UWswFD8ZOj_

Ditto helps you choose the right insurance with unbiased advice and zero spam calls.  Our IRDAI-certified advisors compare policies across insurers — not just one company.  We recommend plans purely based on your needs, age, health profile, and budget.

comments.text:
There is first a 30-day initial waiting period where only accident-related hospitalizations are covered. Beyond that, insurers apply a two-year specific illness or procedure waiting period only for the conditions listed under that clause, and this list varies from insurer to insurer. Pre-existing diseases have a separate waiting period altogether, usually ranging from one to three years depending on the plan and the company.
We’ve discussed about the waiting periods of this plan from 4:08, you can refer to that.
comments.author: @dittoinsurance

comments.text: How it become 40 lakhs ?
comments.author: @vaibhavagarwal3757

comments.text:
HDFC Ergo Optima Secure plan offers coverage as:
1x - Base cover
2x - Secure Benefit (provides additional 1x coverage from day 1)
3x - Plus Benefit (increase the cover by 50% every year up to 100% in 2 years)
4x - Restoration benefit (Available every year after a claim is made in the policy year)

Here’s how it works for a 3-year scenario with a base sum insured (SI) of 10 L:
1st year: Base SI 10 L + Secure Benefit 10 L = Total 20 L
2nd year: Base SI 10 L + Secure Benefit 10 L + Plus Benefit (Bonus) 5 L = Total 25 L
3rd year: Base SI 10 L + Secure Benefit 10 L + Plus Benefit (Bonus) 10 L = Total 30 L

So the ₹10L Sum Insured would increase to ₹30L, not ₹40L, because the 4x benefit means the restoration component.
comments.author: @dittoinsurance

comments.text: Fraud
comments.author: @RAKESHGANNA

comments.text: When amount is restored unlimited times why 2X coverage??? One and the same right
comments.author: @rk-ch5ng

comments.text:
The 2x benefit and the restoration benefit serve two different purposes, and both add value in their own ways.
The 2x benefit doubles your cover from day 1, this automatically increases your available Sum Insured without needing any claim to trigger it.
Restoration, on the other hand, comes into play after a claim, refilling the base Sum Insured so you still have coverage left for future hospitalizations in the same year.
So, the 2x benefit boosts your starting cover immediately, while restoration ensures you don’t run out of cover once a claim is made. Together, they help keep a higher level of protection throughout the policy year.

comments.author: @lavanyag663

comments.text:
Aggregate deductible means the fixed amount you have to pay in a policy year before the insurance company starts paying for claims. You need to cross (or “breach”) this amount once in a policy year, and after that, the remaining eligible claims will be taken care of by the insurer.
Opting for a deductible gives you a discount on premiums — because the insurer’s liability starts only after that limit. How much claim you can use depends on your chosen Sum Insured and the deductible amount.
However, it’s generally not actively recommended to go for a deductible because during an emergency you may not always have the liquidity to first pay the deductible amount — which can create unnecessary stress when treatment is needed urgently. But if you already have another insurance policy (from a different company or employer) that can help you cover the initial deductible amount, then opting for this feature can still work fine.

It provides discounts on lab tests, medicines, tele-consultations, and unlimited doctor consultations with general physicians — but only through partnered facilities in select locations.

We’d recommend connecting with your Ditto advisor or booking a call with us to confirm whether this add-on is available in your area and linked to your policy.

We truly understand how frustrating that experience must have been, especially given your background and understanding of the condition.

However, it's important to note that we cannot intervene in the internal underwriting decisions of the insurer, as plan issuance or denial is entirely based on the company’s risk assessment and health profile evaluation.

That said, we’d strongly recommend getting in touch with our team once again — they can re-check the specifics, and if there’s any scope to provide additional clarification or documentation, they’ll definitely help take it forward if possible - https://ditto.sh/vj9ov0

comments.text: If epilepsy is diagnosed after buying the policy, then yes — it will be covered from the 31st day onwards. But if it was diagnosed before purchasing, then it’ll be treated as a pre-existing condition. In that case, the insurer will go through all the medical records first. If they approve and issue the plan, then epilepsy will be covered — but only after a 3-year waiting period.

comments.text: What about new india assurance
comments.author: @gauravgamer4039

comments.text:
New India Assurance is a public sector insurer, and we generally don’t recommend their plans due to a few limitations. Their policies often come with disease-wise sub-limits, room rent restrictions, and typically lack features like bonus and restoration benefits that are common in more comprehensive private plans.

Additionally, they have a smaller network of hospitals, involve TPAs (Third Party Administrators) in claims — which can delay the process — and also show a higher Incurred Claim Ratio (ICR), which may affect long-term premiums sustainability.

comments.author: @shrideepack

comments.text:
Arshad, you've overlooked a few major points that need to be taken under consideration:

1. I do agree that if you choose a deductible then the premium will be reduced but but but, there is no cashless facility you can avail. You have to go via the reimbursement process.

2. So if a person is in high cash liquidity, it's good for him/her else the meaning of health insurance is somehow not justified.

The reason I said this is because, a heart strain implants cost you 5-10lakh (hypothetically). Now the entire amount must be taken care of by you even if you have the insurance at the time of emergency. Then you go for reimbursement.
So, technically you have to pay the bill out of your pocket and then get the money back.

3. The deductible option you can only change at the time of renewal, and there are again terms and conditions 
3.i. once opted, you must renew the policy at least 5 years in a row. ( No modification in between even renewal - one type of lock in)
3.ii. if opted out you can't add this option in future anymore. Meaning this can be changed once in a lifetime.
3.iii. if you want to opt out, your age must be below 50 years

4. You are young. Have you considered the premium hike in coming years? By the age of 50 or 60 your premium will be unfathomably high.

FYI: hdfc will increase premium every year from 2-4% + it will increase premium by 10-15% whenever there is a medical inflation approved by IRDAI if we are lucky, and it used to happen every 2-3 years.
+ After 60, your premium will be increased each year by 10% depending upon your health conditions no matter what.

If you already considered all the info above, then I must say you have taken a good financial decision , else think thrice and thank me later.

Tip: Forecast your premium till the age of 70-75 or "X" number of age. You will be astonished

comments.text: does restore benefit can be utilised for first claim in a policy year itself, or it will be triggered in second claim only.
comments.author: @sudheershenoy387

comments.text:
The restore benefit gets triggered only after a claim is settled. So if it’s your first policy year and you make a first claim (whether partial or full usage of the sum insured happens), the restore benefit will refill the sum insured after that claim is processed.

This means the restored amount can be used only for any subsequent claim within the same policy year — it cannot be used for the first claim itself.
